published_prior_art.cite: DudikLangfordLi2011: DR contextual-bandit policy evaluation recovers the point target under standard logging/reward-model conditions.

HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 and IshikawaHe2023 give sharp off-policy/confounded contextual-bandit value bounds; KallusZhou2020 gives sharp confounding-robust fixed-policy OPE bounds in infinite-horizon RL; none adds the nominal outcome-regression tube.

KallusZhou2018/KallusZhou2021/KallusZhou2020 vary propensity/confounding sensitivity sets, but I found no stated open-set non-rectangular gap for a paired propensity-divergence plus reward-regression tube.
Manski1990: bounded-outcome no-assumption fill-in bounds for treatment effects.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses closer comparators: KallusZhou2020 on sharp confounding-robust fixed-policy OPE bounds and ZhangXuTan2025/deMSM on simultaneous treatment-probability and outcome-distribution sensitivity constraints; the kernel may survive but the epsilon-gap must be positioned against these.

soundness_flags.label: Assumption 4 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The f-divergence budget is written over all actions while e0 is only required positive on pi support; add absolute-continuity/boundary conventions and a unique-zero condition for f, or explicitly state how zero e0 cells are handled.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Kallus--Zhou 2020 confounding-robust policy evaluation and Zhang--Xu--Tan 2025 deMSM as explicit closest-prior comparisons, explaining why the proposed sup-norm nominal outcome-regression tube is not their outcome-distribution sensitivity constraint.
Tighten the locked assumptions for f-divergence: support of e0, extended-value conventions when e0=0, nonnegativity, and D_f=0 iff the kernels agree.

Replace or supplement U_rect with a rectangular envelope derived from a one-source bound plus an explicit m0-centered outcome tube, so the non-rectangularity gap is not driven by an overly loose diagnostic definition.
Give a concrete two-action, three-outcome numerical witness for Conjecture 2 with strict inequalities, then state the open-set perturbation argument around that witness.

recommended_next_step: Patch the survey and f-divergence conventions, then rerun novelty review before Stage 0.
